description.en: If the character set must be encoded before it can be used in an email header, this attribute will be set to charset.QP (for quoted-printable), charset.BASE64 (for base64 encoding), or charset.SHORTEST for the shortest of QP or BASE64 encoding. Otherwise, it will be None.

excerpt.es: If the character set must be encoded before it can be used in an email header, this attribute will be set to charset.QP (for quoted-printable), charset.BASE64 (for base64 encoding), or charset.SHORTES...
excerpt.en: If the character set must be encoded before it can be used in an email header, this attribute will be set to charset.QP (for quoted-printable), charset.BASE64 (for base64 encoding), or charset.SHORTES...

description.en: Header-encode the string string. The type of encoding (base64 or quoted-printable) will be based on the header_encoding attribute.

excerpt.es: Header-encode the string string. The type of encoding (base64 or quoted-printable) will be based on the header_encoding attribute.
excerpt.en: Header-encode the string string. The type of encoding (base64 or quoted-printable) will be based on the header_encoding attribute.

description.en: Header-encode a string by converting it first to bytes. This is similar to header_encode() except that the string is fit into maximum line lengths as given by the argument maxlengths, which must be an iterator: each element returned from this iterator will provide the next maximum line length.
